sections.subtitle: Povratnički podcast uoči osmog Antunovskog hoda
sections.screenshot_description: Voditelj Marko Petrović otvara podcast i predstavlja gosta fra Stjepana Brčinu, organizatora Antunovskog hoda.
sections.content:
Voditelj Marko Petrović otvara 109. epizodu podcasta udruge *Mladi za domovinu* nakon duže stanke i odluku za 'povratnički' nastup obrazlaže izborom gosta — fra Stjepana Brčine, franjevca koji je već osmu godinu aktivan u pastoralu na zagrebačkom Svetom Duhu i organizator je Antunovskog hoda. Razlog snimanja je konkretan: osmi Antunovski hod održat će se 6. lipnja, a fra Stjepan otkriva da je priprema u finalnoj fazi.

Umjesto da govori samo o logistici, fra Stjepan odmah skreće na duhovnu dimenziju: iza vanjske slike kolone od dvadesetak tisuća mladih koja predvođena nasmiješenim franjevcem hodi prema Bazilici svetog Antuna stoji mukotrpan rad i, prije svega, intenzivna molitvena priprema. 'Mi se još uvijek jako intenzivno duhovno pripremamo, molimo za Antonovski hod, molimo za sve one koji će doći, molimo za zaštitu od zloga', objašnjava. Inzistira da hod nije 'prazno šetanje', nego 'intenzivna duhovna obnova' — i ako ljudi to ne dožive, kako sam kaže, 'džabe smo krečili'.

magisterium.assessment: Izjave fra Stjepana Brčine u potpunosti su usklađene s katoličkim naukom koji hodočašće definira kao snažno duhovno iskustvo, vježbu askeze i put obraćenja.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva uči da je hodočašće 'vježba praktične askeze' i 'unutarnja priprema za promjenu srca', a ne tek puko putovanje. Molitva za zaštitu od zloga izravno se nadovezuje na sedmi zaziv Očenaša, kojim vjernici mole za oslobođenje od zasjeda đavla i svih zala koja štete spasenju duše.

citations.document_title: Directory on Popular Piety and the Liturgy

magisterium.assessment_en: The statements of fra Stjepan Brčina are fully aligned with Catholic teaching which defines pilgrimage as a strong spiritual experience, an exercise of asceticism, and a path of conversion.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that pilgrimage is 'an exercise of practical asceticism' and 'an inner preparation for a change of heart', and not just a mere journey. The prayer for protection from evil is directly connected to the seventh petition of the Our Father, by which the faithful pray for deliverance from the snares of the devil and all evils which harm the salvation of the soul.

sections.subtitle_en: Return podcast on the eve of the eighth Antunovski hod
sections.screenshot_description_en: Host Marko Petrović opens the podcast and introduces the guest fra Stjepan Brčina, the organizer of the Antunovski walk.
sections.content_en: Host Marko Petrović opens the 109th episode of the podcast of the association *Mladi za domovinu* after a long break and explains the decision for the 'comeback' appearance with the choice of guest — fra Stjepan Brčina, a Franciscan friar who has been active for the eighth year in pastoral care at Zagreb's Sveti Duh and is the organizer of the Antunovski hod. The reason for recording is concrete: the eighth Antunovski hod will take place on June 6th, and fra Stjepan reveals that the preparation is in the final phase. Instead of talking only about logistics, fra Stjepan immediately turns to the spiritual dimension: behind the external image of a column of about twenty thousand young people led by a smiling Franciscan friar walking towards the Basilica of Saint Anthony stands arduous work and, above all, intense prayer preparation. 'We are still very intensely spiritually preparing, praying for the Antunovski hod, praying for all those who will come, praying for protection from the evil one,' he explains. He insists that the walk is not 'empty strolling', but 'an intense spiritual renewal' — and if people do not experience that, as he himself says, 'we painted for nothing'.

sections.subtitle: Fizički susret — kritika 'online svećenika'
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina objašnjava zašto je fizički susret s ljudima nezamjenjiv u evangelizaciji, unatoč velikoj prisutnosti na medijima.
sections.content:
Voditelj primjećuje da je fra Stjepan vrlo prisutan na medijima — propovijedi se snima, dijele i emitiraju na *Laudato TV* — pa je tim važnije što inzistira na fizičkom susretu. Brčina to tumači gotovo programatski: 'Imate danas hrpu tih propovjednika koji su online, a crkve su im prazne. Dakle, prvo sebi to govorim. Biti hit na internetu, na društvenim mrežama, a na misi ne možeš da skupiš pet ljudi — onda tu nešto ima.'

Ljudi koji dolaze na Antunovski hod, kaže, nisu 'spali s Marsa'. Riječ je o stalnom kontaktu, neprestanoj komunikaciji, godinama susreta po župama i duhovnim obnovama diljem Hrvatske, Bosne i iseljeništva. Više od 300 ljudi sudjeluje u organizaciji ovogodišnjeg hoda — od evangelizacijske zajednice na Svetom Duhu i župe svetog Antuna kao suorganizatora, do mladih koji se sami organiziraju oko prijevoza autobusima iz dijaspore.

magisterium.assessment: Brčinina kritika snažno odražava katolički nauk o nužnosti fizičke prisutnosti i utjelovljenja u pastoralnom radu, naglašavajući da digitalni mediji ne mogu zamijeniti sakramentalnu stvarnost.
concerns: Postoji opasnost od devalvacije sakramenata ako se virtualna prisutnost počne smatrati dostatnom zamjenom za fizičko sudjelovanje.
Digitalni uspjeh može stvoriti privid duhovne plodnosti koji nije potvrđen u konkretnoj župnoj zajednici i sakramentalnom životu.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva uči da na internetu nema sakramenata te da virtualna stvarnost ne može zamijeniti stvarnu prisutnost Krista u Euharistiji i zajedništvo 'od krvi i mesa'. Evangelizacija mora voditi ljude od cyberspacea prema stvarnoj zajednici i osobnom susretu koji je temelj kršćanske vjere ukorijenjene u Utjelovljenju.
magisterium.assessment_en: Brčina's criticism strongly reflects Catholic teaching on the necessity of physical presence and incarnation in pastoral work, emphasizing that digital media cannot replace sacramental reality.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that on the internet there are no sacraments and that virtual reality cannot replace the real presence of Christ in the Eucharist and community 'of flesh and blood'. Evangelization must lead people from cyberspace towards a real community and a personal encounter which is the foundation of Christian faith rooted in the Incarnation.
concerns_en: There is a danger of devaluation of the sacraments if virtual presence starts to be considered a sufficient substitute for physical participation.
Digital success can create an illusion of spiritual fruitfulness which is not confirmed in a concrete parish community and sacramental life.

sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina explains why the physical encounter with people is irreplaceable in evangelization, despite a large media presence.
sections.content_en: The host notes that fra Stjepan is very present in the media — sermons are recorded, shared, and broadcast on *Laudato TV* — so it is all the more important that he insists on a physical encounter. Brčina explains this almost programmatically: 'Today you have a bunch of those preachers who are online, and their churches are empty. So, first I say this to myself. To be a hit on the internet, on social networks, and at Mass you can't gather five people — then there is something there.' People who come to the Antunovski hod, he says, did not 'fall from Mars'. It is about constant contact, incessant communication, years of encounters in parishes and spiritual renewals throughout Croatia, Bosnia, and the diaspora. More than 300 people are participating in the organization of this year's walk — from the evangelization community at Sveti Duh and the parish of Sveti Antun as co-organizers, to young people who organize themselves around bus transport from the diaspora.

sections.subtitle: Anegdota o kontejnerima: 'Antiša ga je poslao'
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina priča anegdotu o nepoznatom čovjeku koji se javio s Instagrama nudeći kontejnere točno dok je svećenik za doručkom organizirao taj isti problem.
sections.content:
Brčinu pita voditelj o konkretnim trenucima Božje providnosti. On uzvraća anegdotom: razmišljao je da iza Antunovskog hoda u Dubravi ne ostane smeća pa je bogoslovu zadao da organizira veliki kontejner. Bogoslov je zaboravio. Istog jutra, dok je Brčina na doručku ponovno o tome govorio, javio mu se na Instagram nepoznati čovjek koji je prošle godine bio na hodu i u srcu dobio poticaj da pomogne. Imao je — kamione, praonu, vučnu službu. Brčina je oprezno pitao raspolaže li kontejnerima. 'Pa imam i to, ja ću ti to riješiti.'

'Slušaj, Sokole', završava priču, 'ti možda nisi ni svjestan, ali tebe je Antiša poslao jutro sa mnom na adresore.' To je tip svjedočanstva koji se u podcastu vraća u više varijanti — donacije medijskog prostora, jumbo-plakata, splitska ekipa koja sama dovozi hladnjače i osobno dijeli piće — sve kao primjeri 'božje milosti, providnosti i zajedništva'.

magisterium.assessment: Anegdota je u potpunosti usklađena s katoličkim naukom jer ispravno povezuje konkretnu ljudsku potrebu s Božjom providnošću i zagovorom svetaca.
magisterium.enrichment: Katolička tradicija prepoznaje svetog Antuna kao moćnog zagovornika u konkretnim životnim potrebama, što potvrđuje i liturgija Crkve. Ovakvo prepoznavanje 'Božjih prstiju' u svakodnevici odražava autentični sensus fidei vjernika koji u događajima vidi djelovanje žive Crkve i zajedništva svetih.

citations.document_title: Sensus fidei in the life of the Church
General Audience of 10 February 2010: Saint Anthony of Padua

magisterium.assessment_en: The anecdote is fully aligned with Catholic teaching because it correctly connects a concrete human need with God's providence and the intercession of saints.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Catholic tradition recognizes Saint Anthony as a powerful intercessor in concrete life needs, which is also confirmed by the liturgy of the Church. Such recognition of 'God's fingers' in everyday life reflects the authentic sensus fidei of the faithful who see in events the action of the living Church and the communion of saints.

sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina tells an anecdote about an unknown man who contacted from Instagram offering containers exactly while the priest was organizing that same problem at breakfast.
sections.content_en: The host asks Brčina about concrete moments of God's providence. He responds with an anecdote: he was thinking that no trash should remain after the Antunovski walk in Dubrava, so he tasked the seminarian with organizing a large container. The seminarian forgot. That same morning, while Brčina was talking about it again at breakfast, an unknown man contacted him on Instagram who had been at the walk last year and received an impulse in his heart to help. He had — trucks, a car wash, a towing service. Brčina cautiously asked if he had containers. 'Well, I have that too, I will take care of it for you.' 'Listen, Sokole,' he finishes the story, 'you might not even be aware, but Antiša sent you this morning with me to the addressers.' This is the type of testimony that returns in the podcast in multiple variants — donations of media space, jumbo posters, a Split team that brings refrigerated trucks themselves and personally distributes drinks — all as examples of 'God's grace, providence, and community'.

sections.subtitle: Apostoli su izlazili na trgove — javno svjedočenje kao milost
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina povlači paralelu između današnjih sudionika hoda i apostola koji su išli na trgove svjedočiti Krista.
sections.content:
Na pitanje zašto bi netko uopće trebao doći, Brčina prvo ističe evangelizacijski karakter hoda. Vjernici se navikavaju biti vjernici u crkvi i u svojoj sobi, ali javno svjedočenje vjere mnogi nikada nisu probali. 'Sjetimo se početaka kršćanstva, sjetimo se apostola koji su išli na trgove. Ne kažem da trebamo u tom obliku, ali hoću reći da je to totalno drugačiji osjećaj — kao da dobiješ neku milost posebnu kad svjedočiš javno negdje.'

Govori iz osobnog iskustva ulične evangelizacije: 'Nije isti osjećaj kao kad stojiš u crkvi. Apsolutno nije isti, ali dobiješ neku posebnu milinu i blagoslov.' Drugi razlog dolaska je sama struktura hoda — Brčina inzistira da to nije šetnja nego 'intenzivna duhovna obnova' u kojoj se srce postupno otvara kroz pjesmu, nagovor, ispovijed, misu i hod. 'Ne treba doći samo na misu. Zato što ima koncept tako da od samog početka čovjeku kroz taj program — ljudi možda toga nisu ni svjesni — se polako otvara srce.'

magisterium.assessment: Izjava je u potpunosti usklađena s katoličkim naukom jer naglašava dužnost svih krštenika da javno svjedoče vjeru, što je temeljno poslanje Crkve. Brčinovo iskustvo 'posebne milosti' odražava teološku stvarnost da Duh Sveti osnažuje vjernike za naviještanje.
magisterium.enrichment: Drugi vatikanski sabor u dekretu 'Ad Gentes' ističe da su svi kršćani dužni primjerom života i svjedočanstvom riječi očitovati 'novoga čovjeka'. Dokument 'Evangelii Nuntiandi' potvrđuje da je svjedočenje prvi i nezamjenjiv način evangelizacije, jer suvremeni čovjek radije sluša svjedoke nego učitelje.
magisterium.assessment_en: The statement is fully aligned with Catholic teaching because it emphasizes the duty of all baptized persons to publicly witness to the faith, which is the fundamental mission of the Church. Brčina's experience of 'special grace' reflects the theological reality that the Holy Spirit empowers the faithful for proclamation.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Second Vatican Council in the decree 'Ad Gentes' emphasizes that all Christians are obliged by the example of life and the testimony of word to manifest the 'new man'. The document 'Evangelii Nuntiandi' confirms that testimony is the first and irreplaceable way of evangelization, because modern man prefers to listen to witnesses rather than teachers.

sections.subtitle_en: The Apostles went out into the squares — public witness as grace
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina draws a parallel between today's participants of the walk and the apostles who went to the squares to witness Christ.
sections.content_en: To the question why someone should come at all, Brčina first emphasizes the evangelization character of the walk. The faithful get used to being faithful in church and in their room, but many have never tried public witnessing of faith. 'Let us remember the beginnings of Christianity, let us remember the apostles who went to the squares. I am not saying that we need to in that form, but I want to say that it is a totally different feeling — as if you receive some special grace when you witness publicly somewhere.' He speaks from personal experience of street evangelization: 'It is not the same feeling as when you stand in church. It is absolutely not the same, but you receive some special tenderness and blessing.' The second reason for coming is the very structure of the walk — Brčina insists that it is not a stroll but 'an intensive spiritual renewal' in which the heart gradually opens through song, exhortation, confession, Mass, and the walk. 'One should not come only for Mass. Because it has a concept such that from the very beginning, through that program — people might not even be aware of it — the heart slowly opens to a person.'

sections.subtitle: Đavolova vremenska prognoza i duhovna lijenost
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina kritički govori o izgovorima kojima vjernici izbjegavaju duhovne događaje.
sections.content:
Brčina ne idealizira sudionike. Mnogi bi htjeli blagoslove i promjene, ali, kako kaže, 'dignuli bi sve četiri kornjača i da samo Bog puše i daje'. Iz iskustva svjedoči da đavao 'uvijek nađe neki razlog' da netko ne dođe: vrijeme, prevoz, 'ne idu moji, šta ću sam'.

Vrlo izravno ide na vjernike koji se srame javno posvjedočiti da su katolici. 'Ako smo bojažljivi, to znači da je pitanje koje treba postaviti — kakav je taj odnos s Bogom, koji je duh u mom srcu. Jer nemoguće je živjeti ispravno svoju vjeru, imati iskustvo Boga i šutjeti. To je nemoguće.' Apostoli su išli toliko hrabro da su izlazili na ulicu sa sviješću da će izgubiti život za Isusa, pa retorički pita kako današnji kršćani mogu 'pasivno šutjeti, ne svjedočiti'. Odgovor je oštar: 'Nešto nije u redu, ne gori srce.'

magisterium.assessment: Tekst je teološki utemeljen jer ispravno identificira acediju kao duhovnu tromost koja se protivi radosti u Bogu i naglašava nužnost ljudske suradnje s milošću.
magisterium.enrichment: Acedija se definira kao 'podnevni demon' koji gasi revnost za duhovni razvoj i vodi do averzije prema djelovanju (taedium operandi). Katolički nauk naglašava sinergiju u kojoj je pristanak volje na milost i sam dar milosti, ali zahtijeva aktivan ljudski napor u evangelizaciji.

citations.document_title: General Audience of 14 February 2024 - Vices and Virtues 8. Acedia

magisterium.assessment_en: The text is theologically founded because it correctly identifies acedia as spiritual sloth which opposes joy in God and emphasizes the necessity of human cooperation with grace.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Acedia is defined as the 'midday demon' which extinguishes zeal for spiritual development and leads to aversion to action (taedium operandi). Catholic teaching emphasizes a synergy in which there is the assent of the will to grace and the gift of grace itself, but requires active human effort in evangelization.

sections.subtitle_en: Devil's weather forecast and spiritual laziness
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina critically speaks about excuses with which believers avoid spiritual events.
sections.content_en: Brčina does not idealize the participants. Many would want blessings and changes, but, as he says, 'they would lift all four turtle legs and let only God blow and give'. From experience, he testifies that the devil 'always finds some reason' for someone not to come: weather, transport, 'mine are not going, what will I do alone'. He very directly addresses the faithful who are ashamed to publicly testify that they are Catholics. 'If we are timid, that means the question to ask is — what kind of relationship is that with God, what spirit is in my heart. Because it is impossible to live one's faith correctly, to have an experience of God and be silent. That is impossible.' The apostles went so bravely that they went out into the street with the awareness that they would lose their lives for Jesus, so he rhetorically asks how today's Christians can 'passively be silent, not testify'. The answer is sharp: 'Something is not right, the heart is not burning.'

sections.subtitle: Nema duhovnosti bez sakramenata
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina sustavno tumači zašto su sakramenti, posebice euharistija i euharistijsko klanjanje, neizostavni za duhovni život.
sections.content:
Središnja teza prvog dijela razgovora je da 'nema duhovnosti bez sakramenata' — i Brčina to ponavlja s naglaskom: 'Kad netko priča, to je mantra prazna. Dakle, ne možemo se mi oduprijeti Sotoni, đavlu, bez milosti sakramenata.' Bog je živ u sakramentima, oni krijepe i jačaju, a sve drugo je 'šuplja priča'.

Kao primjer pozitivne promjene navodi euharistijsko klanjanje. Prije deset godina, kaže, bila je to gotovo zaboravljena pobožnost koja se pripisivala samo starim vjernicima. 'Sada pogledaj — nema nijednog plakata gdje netko nešto organizira duhovno, a da nema klanjanja. I na ta klanjanja najviše idu mladi.'

> **Magisterium uskladenost:** Brčinin nauk doslovno odgovara onome što Crkva uči. Katekizam Katoličke Crkve (br. 1378-1380) ističe da je euharistijsko klanjanje izraz vjere u Kristovu stvarnu prisutnost, a Ivan Pavao II. u enciklici *Ecclesia de Eucharistia* (25) klanjanje naziva 'neprocjenjivim blagom'. Tridentski sabor (XIII. zasjedanje) potvrđuje da je u euharistiji Krist prisutan 'istinski, stvarno i bitno'.

magisterium.assessment: Izjava je snažno usklađena s katoličkim naukom o sakramentalnoj ekonomiji kao nužnom putu priopćavanja plodova Kristova otkupljenja. Ispravno naglašava da sakramenti djeluju ex opere operato i pružaju objektivnu milost potrebnu za pobjedu u duhovnom boju protiv zla.
concerns: Isključivost tvrdnje da je sve izvan sakramenata 'šuplja priča' može zanemariti vrijednost sakramentalija i osobnih čina pobožnosti koji djeluju ex opere operantis.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva uči da, iako su sakramenti vrhunac, Božja milost nije ograničena samo na njih te se može primiti i kroz savršeno pokajanje ili 'krštenje želje' kada sakrament nije dostupan. Duhovni život je 'dijaloški' proces u kojem sakramentalna milost zahtijeva suradnju vjere i budnost kako bi donijela puni plod u svakodnevici.

citations.document_title: The Reciprocity between Faith and Sacraments

citations.document_author: Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith

magisterium.assessment_en: The statement is strongly aligned with Catholic teaching on sacramental economy as a necessary path of communicating the fruits of Christ's redemption. It correctly emphasizes that sacraments act ex opere operato and provide objective grace necessary for victory in the spiritual battle against evil.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that, although the sacraments are the culmination, God's grace is not limited only to them and can also be received through perfect repentance or 'baptism of desire' when the sacrament is not available. Spiritual life is a 'dialogical' process in which sacramental grace requires the cooperation of faith and vigilance to bear full fruit in daily life.
concerns_en: The exclusivity of the claim that everything outside of the sacraments is an 'empty story' can disregard the value of sacramentals and personal acts of piety which act ex opere operantis.

sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina systematically explains why sacraments, especially Eucharist and Eucharistic adoration, are indispensable for spiritual life.
sections.content_en:
The central thesis of the first part of the conversation is that 'there is no spirituality without sacraments' — and Brčina repeats this with emphasis: 'When someone talks, it's an empty mantra. Therefore, we cannot resist Satan, the devil, without the grace of the sacraments.' God is alive in the sacraments, they strengthen and empower, and everything else is 'empty talk'.

As an example of positive change, he mentions Eucharistic adoration. Ten years ago, he says, it was an almost forgotten devotion attributed only to old believers. 'Now look — there isn't a single poster where someone organizes something spiritual without adoration. And it is mostly young people who go to these adorations.'

> **Magisterium alignment:** Brčina's teaching literally corresponds to what the Church teaches. The Catechism of the Catholic Church (nos. 1378-1380) emphasizes that Eucharistic adoration is an expression of faith in Christ's real presence, and John Paul II in the encyclical *Ecclesia de Eucharistia* (25) calls adoration 'an inestimable treasure'. The Council of Trent (Session XIII) confirms that in the Eucharist Christ is present 'truly, really, and substantially'.

sections.subtitle: Izričita zapovijed: 'tko jede tijelo moje, ima život vječni'
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina argumentira da euharistija nije opcija nego Kristova izričita zapovijed iz Posljednje večere.
sections.content:
Brčina pomno odbacuje suvremenu 'privatnu vjeru' — pristup 'ja sam vjernik, ali ne moram u crkvu, gledam misu na televizoru, ispovijedam se u sobi'. To, kaže, 'Isus nije tražio. Isus je tražio da se sjedinimo s njim.'

Ključni argument gradi na Posljednjoj večeri: 'Ako je Bog Izraelce hranio manom u pustinji 40 godina, ako je nama Isus rekao na Posljednjoj večeri — znači, nije nam dao opciju, nije rekao eto kad bude lijepo vrijeme, kad vam se bude dalo, kad ne budete umorni, idite jedite i pijte tijelo moje — nego izričita zapovijed: tko jede i pije tijelo i krv moju, ima u sebi život vječni. Točka.'

Problem mnogih, kaže, je 'neznanje': ljudi neće na misu zbog 'ovog ili onog svećenika', ne shvaćajući da euharistija ne postaje sveta zbog svetosti svećenika. 'Jedan je svećenik prinio žrtvu za sve. To je Isus.' Citira riječi posvete: 'Ne kažemo Bože, posveti po mojoj svetosti, nego se obraćamo: Bože, izvore svake svetosti, snagom Duha Svetoga posveti ove darove.'

magisterium.assessment: Izjave su teološki snažne i uglavnom usklađene s katoličkim naukom o nužnosti euharistije i objektivnosti sakramenta, no zahtijevaju preciznije nijansiranje uloge svećenika.
concerns: Tvrdnja da svetost euharistije 'ne ovisi o svećeniku' jer je Isus jedini prinio žrtvu može se pogrešno protumačiti kao nijekanje nužnosti zaređenog ministra koji djeluje 'in persona Christi'.
Odbacivanje 'privatne vjere' ne smije previdjeti mogućnost 'duhovne pričesti' ili krštenja željom u izvanrednim okolnostima.
magisterium.enrichment: Potrebno je naglasiti da, iako je Krist jedini Veliki svećenik, on djeluje kroz zaređenog svećenika koji u trenutku posvećenja nastupa 'in persona Christi', što je nužno za valjanost sakramenta. Također, nauk o 'ex opere operato' jamči objektivnu prisutnost milosti neovisno o moralnosti svećenika, ali plodovi u vjerniku ovise o njegovoj dispoziciji ('ex opere operantis').

citations.document_title: Christ's Male Sexuality and Acting In Persona Christi

magisterium.assessment_en: The statements are theologically strong and largely aligned with Catholic teaching on the necessity of the Eucharist and the objectivity of the sacrament, but they require more precise nuance regarding the role of the priest.
magisterium.enrichment_en: It is necessary to emphasize that, although Christ is the only High Priest, he acts through the ordained priest who at the moment of consecration acts 'in persona Christi', which is necessary for the validity of the sacrament. Also, the teaching of 'ex opere operato' guarantees the objective presence of grace independently of the morality of the priest, but the fruits in the believer depend on his disposition ('ex opere operantis').
concerns_en: The claim that the sanctity of the Eucharist 'does not depend on the priest' because Jesus alone offered the sacrifice can be misinterpreted as a denial of the necessity of an ordained minister who acts 'in persona Christi'.
The rejection of 'private faith' must not overlook the possibility of 'spiritual communion' or baptism of desire in extraordinary circumstances.

sections.subtitle_en: Explicit commandment: 'who eats my body, has eternal life'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina argues that the Eucharist is not an option but Christ's explicit commandment from the Last Supper.
sections.content_en: Brčina carefully rejects contemporary 'private faith' — the approach 'I am a believer, but I don't have to go to church, I watch Mass on TV, I confess in my room'. That, he says, 'Jesus did not ask for. Jesus asked us to unite with Him'. He builds his key argument on the Last Supper: 'If God fed the Israelites with manna in the desert for 40 years, if Jesus told us at the Last Supper — meaning, He did not give us an option, He did not say, 'well, when the weather is nice, when you feel like it, when you are not tired, go eat and drink My body' — but an explicit command: whoever eats and drinks My body and blood has eternal life within him. Period'. The problem of many, he says, is 'ignorance': people won't go to Mass because of 'this or that priest', not realizing that the Eucharist does not become holy because of the priest's holiness. 'One priest offered the sacrifice for all. That is Jesus'. He quotes the words of consecration: 'We do not say God, consecrate by my holiness, but we address: God, source of all holiness, by the power of the Holy Spirit consecrate these gifts'.

sections.subtitle: Sotonisti znaju koja je hostija posvećena
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina koristi snažan argument iz prakse sotonizma kao dokaz stvarne prisutnosti.
sections.content:
Najsnažniji argument u korist stvarne prisutnosti Brčina nalazi na obrnutoj strani duhovne stvarnosti. 'Kako oni znaju koja je posvećena hostija, a mi kršćani, katolici, ne znamo? Ili nemamo to strahopoštovanje? Zašto oni kradu posvećene hostije? Zašto ne odu u Verbum i kupe neposvećene na kile?'

Odgovor mu je da sotonisti 'točno znaju koja je posvećena'. Ako oni iz mržnje prepoznaju ono što je u hostiji, kršćani bi tim više morali shvatiti što sami primaju. 'Pravo kršćanina koji blaguje tijelo i krv Kristovu, sotonist poznaje na kilometre.' Iz toga slijedi i njegova praktična pouka: 'Mi ne možemo raditi nikakvu proklamaciju ni zaštitu ukoliko nismo opremljeni sakramentima.'

magisterium.assessment: Argument se snažno oslanja na katolički nauk o stvarnoj prisutnosti i transubstancijaciji, ali koristi anegdotalnu demonologiju kao dokaznu metodu umjesto isključivo očiju vjere.
concerns: Korištenje sotonističkih praksi kao 'dokaza' za dogmu može zasjeniti činjenicu da se stvarna prisutnost spoznaje razumom prosvijetljenim vjerom (oculi fidei), a ne empirijskim ili demonskim očitovanjima.
Postoji rizik od davanja prevelikog autoriteta demonskim uvidima u katehezi, što Crkva poziva na oprez i kritičku procjenu.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva potvrđuje da se posvećenjem kruh i vino pretvaraju u Tijelo i Krv Kristovu, što sotonisti profaniraju pod 'đavolskim nadahnućem'. Iako tradicija bilježi da demoni drhte pred Presvetim, vjera je 'dokaz nevidljivoga' koji nadilazi osjetilnu ili magijsku spoznaju.

citations.document_title: Interpretationes Authenticae - Safeguarding the Bread of Life

citations.document_author: Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith

magisterium.assessment_en: The argument strongly relies on Catholic teaching on the real presence and transubstantiation, but uses anecdotal demonology as a proof method instead of exclusively the eyes of faith.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church confirms that by consecration bread and wine are transformed into the Body and Blood of Christ, which satanists profane under 'diabolical inspiration'. Although tradition records that demons tremble before the Blessed Sacrament, faith is 'the proof of the unseen' which transcends sensory or magical knowledge.
concerns_en: The use of satanic practices as 'proof' for dogma can overshadow the fact that the real presence of understanding is perceived by reason enlightened by faith (oculi fidei), and not by empirical or demonic manifestations.
There is a risk of giving too much authority to demonic insights in catechesis, which the Church calls for caution and critical evaluation.

sections.subtitle_en: Satanists know which host is consecrated
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina uses a strong argument from the practice of Satanism as proof of real presence.
sections.content_en: The strongest argument in favor of the real presence Brčina finds on the opposite side of spiritual reality. 'How do they know which host is consecrated, and we Christians, Catholics, do not know? Or do we not have that reverence? Why do they steal consecrated hosts? Why do they not go to Verbum and buy unconsecrated ones by the kilo?' His answer is that satanists 'know exactly which one is consecrated'. If they, out of hatred, recognize what is in the host, Christians should all the more understand what they themselves receive. 'The right of a Christian who partakes of the Body and Blood of Christ, a satanist recognizes for kilometers.' From that also follows his practical teaching: 'We cannot make any proclamation or protection unless we are equipped with the sacraments.'

sections.subtitle: Klanjanje kao sunčanje pred živim Bogom
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina uspoređuje euharistijsko klanjanje sa sunčanjem na plaži — promjena se događa neovisno o tome osjećaš li je odmah.
sections.content:
Za klanjanje uvodi vjerojatno najpamtljiviju usporedbu cijelog razgovora: sunčanje. 'Mi idemo na plažu, namažemo se kremama, cijeli dan si tamo i imaš osjećaj da nisi nikakvu promjenu doživio. Odeš navečer se istušuraš — ili si potamnio ili si pocrvenio, ali vidiš promjenu. To je klanjanje. Biti pred živim Bogom. Nemoguće da nije utjecalo na tebe.'

Ne treba, kaže, plakati niti tražiti vidljive znakove. Milost se 'polako spušta', a snaga dolazi kad zatreba — 'kad ti bude trebala snaga da se odupreš, sotoni ili nečemu drugom'. Citira poznatu izreku jednog svetca: 'Tko može klečati pred Bogom, može stajati pred bilo kim.' Kao paradigmatski primjer navodi Majku Tereziju koja je ujutro, prije nego što bi krenula u rad sa siromasima, 'sat vremena išla pred presveti oltarski sakrament. I tek bi onda imala ljubav i snage cijeli dan za bolesne, siromašne i ostavljene.'

> **Magisterium uskladenost:** KKC 1380 prenosi riječi Ivana Pavla II.: 'Crkva i svijet imaju veliku potrebu za euharistijskim štovanjem. Isus nas čeka u ovom sakramentu ljubavi. Neka naše klanjanje nikada ne prestane.'

magisterium.assessment: Tekst je u potpunosti usklađen s katoličkim naukom, naglašavajući objektivnu snagu Kristove prisutnosti i neraskidivu vezu između kontemplacije i djelatne ljubavi.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva uči da klanjanje produbljuje plodove pričesti i omogućuje vjernicima da postanu 'kontemplativci u akciji', prenoseći Kristovu prisutnost u svakodnevni život. Klečanje se smatra izrazom kršćanske kulture koji priznaje Božje veličanstvo i priprema tijelo za uskrsnuće.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text is fully aligned with Catholic teaching, emphasizing the objective power of Christ's presence and the indissoluble link between contemplation and active love.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that Eucharistic adoration deepens the fruits of Communion and enables the faithful to become 'contemplatives in action', carrying Christ's presence into daily life. Kneeling is considered an expression of Christian culture that acknowledges God's majesty and prepares the body for resurrection.

sections.subtitle_en: Eucharistic adoration as sunbathing before the living God
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina compares Eucharistic adoration with sunbathing on the beach — change happens regardless of whether you feel it immediately.
sections.content_en:
For Eucharistic adoration, he introduces probably the most memorable comparison of the entire conversation: sunbathing. 'We go to the beach, we put on creams, you are there all day and you have the feeling that you haven't experienced any change. You go in the evening, you shower — either you have tanned or you have reddened, but you see a change. That is Eucharistic adoration. To be before the living God. It is impossible that it has not affected you.'

One should not, he says, cry nor seek visible signs. Grace 'slowly descends', and strength comes when needed — 'when you will need strength to resist, Satan or something else'. He quotes a well-known saying of a saint: 'Whoever can kneel before God, can stand before anyone.' As a paradigmatic example, he mentions Mother Teresa who in the morning, before she would start working with the poor, 'went for an hour before the Blessed Sacrament. And only then would she have love and strength all day for the sick, the poor, and the abandoned.'

> **Magisterium alignment:** CCC 1380 conveys the words of John Paul II: 'The Church and the world have a great need for Eucharistic adoration. Jesus awaits us in this sacrament of love. May our adoration never cease.'

iterations.theme_en: Antunovski hod as intensive spiritual renewal and the indispensable role of the sacraments

iterations.theme: Osobna molitva srcem, Božja riječ, zajednica i izazovi pastorala mladih

sections.subtitle: Jutarnja molitva kao duhovna zaštita prije izlaska u dan
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina objašnjava analogiju jutarnje higijene i jutarnje duhovne zaštite.
sections.content:
Voditelj prebacuje fokus na osobnu molitvu i pita misli li Brčina da ljudi previše hrle obavezama, a zaboravljaju 'napajanje'. Brčina odgovara analogijom svakodnevne higijene: nezamislivo je izaći iz kuće ne opravši zube i ne sredivši se. 'A zamisli to u duhovnom kontekstu, kad mi ujutro tako izletimo, nezaštićeni. Juriš na posao, juriš negdje — čime se ti zaštititi?'

Molitvu opisuje kao oružje protiv konkretnih duhova s kojima čovjek susreće: 'Mi živimo okruženi duhovnim svijetom, različitim duhovima — duhom bluda, duhom psovke, duhom nemira, duhom straha, duhom ovim, duhom onim.' Konkretna jutarnja molitva može biti jednostavna: 'Bože, daj mi strpljivosti danas, daj mi ljubavi, daj mi milost Duha Svetoga, daj mi mudrosti da vodim taj sastanak.' Plodovi su praktični: 'Nećete promijeniti radne kolege, ali ćete imati više ljubavi za njih. Nećete promijeniti šefa, ali ćete biti snažniji izdržati njegove kritike.'

magisterium.assessment: Brčinina analogija i molitva u potpunosti su usklađene s katoličkom tradicijom koja jutarnju molitvu vidi kao nužnu posvetu dana i zaštitu u duhovnom boju.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva uči da jutarnja molitva 'posvećuje prve pokrete uma i srca' Bogu, služeći kao lijek protiv grijeha i sredstvo za zadobivanje pomoći u dnevnim aktivnostima. Darovi Duha Svetoga, koje Brčina spominje, trajne su dispozicije koje vjernika čine poslušnim Božjim nadahnućima u borbi protiv poroka.

citations.document_title: General Instruction on the Liturgy of the Hours
General Audience of 25 September 2024 - The Holy Spirit, our ally

magisterium.assessment_en: Brčina's analogy and prayer are fully aligned with Catholic tradition which sees morning prayer as a necessary consecration of the day and protection in the spiritual battle.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that morning prayer 'sanctifies the first movements of the mind and heart' to God, serving as a remedy against sins and a means for obtaining help in daily activities. The Gifts of the Holy Spirit, which Brčina mentions, are permanent dispositions that make the believer obedient to God's inspirations in the fight against vices.

sections.subtitle_en: Morning prayer as spiritual protection before going out into the day
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina explains the analogy of morning hygiene and morning spiritual protection.
sections.content_en: The host shifts the focus to personal prayer and asks if Brčina thinks that people rush too much into obligations and forget 'recharging'. Brčina responds with an analogy of daily hygiene: it is unimaginable to leave the house without brushing one's teeth and getting ready. 'And imagine that in a spiritual context, when we rush out like that in the morning, unprotected. You rush to work, you rush somewhere — with what will you protect yourself?' He describes prayer as a weapon against concrete spirits that a person encounters: 'We live surrounded by a spiritual world, by different spirits — the spirit of lust, the spirit of cursing, the spirit of unrest, the spirit of fear, this spirit, that spirit.' A concrete morning prayer can be simple: 'God, give me patience today, give me love, give me the grace of the Holy Spirit, give me wisdom to lead this meeting.' The fruits are practical: 'You will not change your work colleagues, but you will have more love for them. You will not change your boss, but you will be stronger to endure his criticisms.'

sections.subtitle: Moliti srcem, a ne 'na pamet'
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina kritizira mehaničko izgovaranje naučenih molitava i objašnjava ulogu Duha Svetoga u molitvi.
sections.content:
Voditelj otvoreno priznaje da ga ovo poglavlje osobno zanima jer je u svojoj knjizi *Iz sobe za svakoga* puno pisao o molitvi. Pita kako 'konstruirati' molitvu da ne bude robotizirana. Brčina odgovara teološki: 'Protagonist molitve svake prave jest Duh Sveti. Mi molimo — to je nam dana milost Duha Svetoga, kako kaže Pismo, »u nama se zauzima neizrecivim uzdasima«. I zato nam je potrebna milost Duha Svetoga da on moli u nama.'

Kad Duh Sveti vodi, kaže, 'molitva ti nije teška, nije naporna, nije dosadna, nije molitva koju mjeriš po minutama'. Nekad će biti deset minuta, nekad tri — 'nebitno je, bitno je srce'.

Najoštrija kritika ide na automatizam: 'Tužno je da jedan svećenik danas ne zna sklopiti dvije rečenice, spontano zahvaliti Bogu.' Citira anonimnog sveca: 'Ako te ne mijenja ta tvoja molitva, onda mijenjaj ti nju.' Ilustrira anegdotom — od djetinjstva u Hercegovini čuo je ljude koji mole krunicu i kažu 'Zdravo Marijo, milosti puna…' pa nastave dalje gutajući riječi. To, kaže, 'nije molitva — to je automatizam'.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst je izvrsno usklađen s katoličkim naukom, vjerno interpretirajući ulogu Duha Svetoga iz Rim 8,26 kao onoga koji potiče i oblikuje naše molitvene želje. Naglasak na 'molitvi srca' nasuprot pukom automatizmu izravno odražava učenje Katekizma o nužnosti nutarnje prisutnosti u vokalnoj molitvi (KKC 2700-2704).
magisterium.enrichment: Duh Sveti se naziva 'učiteljem molitve' i 'artizanom Božjih djela' koji u nama budi čežnju za nebeskim dobrima. Crkva uči da vokalna molitva, poput krunice, postaje osobna tek kada je um prožet vjerom, a srce svjesno Onoga kome se obraćamo, čime se izbjegava 'prazno brbljanje'.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text is excellently aligned with Catholic teaching, faithfully interpreting the role of the Holy Spirit from Rom 8:26 as the one who inspires and shapes our prayer desires. The emphasis on 'prayer of the heart' as opposed to mere automatism directly reflects the teaching of the Catechism on the necessity of inner presence in vocal prayer (CCC 2700-2704).
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Holy Spirit is called 'teacher of prayer' and 'artisan of God's works' who awakens in us longing for heavenly goods. The Church teaches that vocal prayer, like the Rosary, becomes personal only when the mind is permeated by faith, and the heart is conscious of Him to Whom we address ourselves, thereby avoiding 'empty babbling'.

sections.subtitle_en: To pray with heart, and not 'by rote'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina criticizes the mechanical recitation of memorized prayers and explains the role of the Holy Spirit in prayer.
sections.content_en: The host openly admits that this chapter personally interests him because in his book *From the Room for Everyone* he wrote a lot about prayer. He asks how to 'construct' prayer so that it is not robotic. Brčina responds theologically: 'The protagonist of every true prayer is the Holy Spirit. We pray — that is the grace of the Holy Spirit given to us, as Scripture says, »He intercedes in us with inexpressible groanings«. And that is why we need the grace of the Holy Spirit for Him to pray in us.' When the Holy Spirit leads, he says, 'your prayer is not difficult, it is not strenuous, it is not boring, it is not a prayer you measure by minutes'. Sometimes it will be ten minutes, sometimes three — 'it is irrelevant, the heart is what matters'. The sharpest criticism goes to automatism: 'It is sad that a priest today does not know how to put together two sentences, to spontaneously thank God.' He quotes an anonymous saint: 'If that prayer of yours does not change you, then you change it.' He illustrates with an anecdote — from childhood in Hercegovina he heard people who pray the Rosary and say 'Hail Mary, full of grace…' and then continue swallowing words. That, he says, 'is not prayer — that is automatism'.

sections.subtitle: Prazna čaša prije punjenja — odbaciti pa primiti
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina opisuje psihološki obrazac ljudi koji 'pune' molitvu zahtjevima, ali ništa ne otpuste.
sections.content:
Brčina nudi vrlo konkretan obrazac molitve: 'Ne može Bog uliti ništa u ovo srce ako ja nisam prvo ispraznio nešto.' Ljudi dolaze opterećeni problemima, križevima, bolestima, mukama — i samo dodaju, traže, nadodaju. 'Prvo izbaci sve iz srca. Imenuj — ako su to neke emocije, strahovi, duhovi, što god — evo, Bože, tebi predajem.' Tek onda dolazi traženje: 'Ako te bio strah, daj mi Bože dar vjere; ako mi treba strpljivost i ljubav, zazovi ih.'

Najiskrenija molitva, kaže, je 'vapaj iz srca' kad čovjek dođe 'do zida' i više ne zna što će. 'Ono je došlo do dna i onda kad više ne znaš što ćeš, onda si kriknuo srcem napokon — jer nije kraj došao, to nije loše. Otvorio si to srce.'

magisterium.assessment: Tekst je u potpunosti usklađen s katoličkim naukom o molitvi kao predanju i siromaštvu duha, naglašavajući nužnost unutarnjeg pražnjenja za prihvaćanje Božje milosti.
magisterium.enrichment: Koncept 'pražnjenja' odražava kristološku kenozu (samopražnjenje) iz Fil 2,7, gdje se Krist lišio slave kako bi primio obličje sluge. Katekizam potvrđuje da je kontemplativna molitva 'ponizno predanje' volji Oca, dok se istinska prošnja rađa iz vjere koja 'baca sve tjeskobe na Njega'.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text is fully aligned with Catholic teaching on prayer as surrender and poverty of spirit, emphasizing the necessity of inner emptying for receiving God's grace.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The concept of 'emptying' reflects Christological kenosis (self-emptying) from Phil 2:7, where Christ deprived Himself of glory to receive the form of a servant. The Catechism confirms that contemplative prayer is a 'humble surrender' to the will of the Father, while true petition is born from faith that 'casts all anxieties on Him'.

sections.subtitle_en: Empty glass before filling — to discard then to receive
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina describes the psychological pattern of people who 'fill' prayer with demands, but release nothing.
sections.content_en: Brčina offers a very concrete pattern of prayer: 'God cannot pour anything into this heart if I have not first emptied something.' People come burdened with problems, crosses, illnesses, torments — and they only add, ask, add more. 'First cast everything out of your heart. Name it — if these are some emotions, fears, spirits, whatever — behold, God, I surrender them to You.' Only then comes the asking: 'If you were afraid, give me, God, the gift of faith; if I need patience and love, call upon them.' The most sincere prayer, he says, is 'a cry from the heart' when a person comes 'to a wall' and no longer knows what to do. 'It has reached the bottom and then when you no longer know what you will do, then you finally cried out with your heart — because the end has not come, that is not bad. You have opened that heart.'

sections.subtitle: Čišćenje slike o Bogu kroz Pismo — metoda korak po korak
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina opisuje konkretnu metodu osobnog čitanja Evanđelja koja odgovara klasičnoj lectio divini.
sections.content:
Voditelj pita kako čitati Pismo i Brčina nudi metodu koja u svemu odgovara klasičnoj *lectio divini*, iako je ne imenuje. Prvo: 'Božja riječ nisu novine niti knjiga. Lijepo se pomoliš Duhu Svetomu, ideš pažljivo, pročitaš jednom, pa se vratiš još jednom, pa drugi i treći put ako treba.' Drugo: 'Osluškuj — hoće li ti na tom tekstu zaigrati srce, možda na nekoj riječi, možda na cijeloj rečenici, možda na cijelom kontekstu.' Treće: 'Vrati se, pitaj — Bože, što ova riječ meni govori? Zašto mi je srce sad tu zastalo? Što mi želiš reći po ovoj riječi?' I četvrto: 'Uzmi tu riječ i napravi od nje molitvu zahvale. I onda uzmi tu riječ i cijeli dan živi s njom.'

Opetovano naglašava da Pismo 'najbolje čisti sliku Boga ili ono što smo mi zamišljali da Bog jest. Sad mi privatno možemo zamišljati Boga ovako, onako, možda su nam utisnuli sliku u djetinjstvu — ali treba slušati Božju riječ jer je tamo objavljena slika Boga.'

> **Magisterium uskladenost:** Metoda koju Brčina opisuje strukturalno je *lectio divina* (čitanje – meditacija – molitva – kontemplacija/življenje), preporučena u posinodalnoj apostolskoj pobudnici Benedikta XVI. *Verbum Domini* (br. 86-87) i u apostolskoj konstituciji *Vultum Dei quaerere* Pape Franje.

magisterium.assessment: Opisana metoda u potpunosti je usklađena s crkvenim naukom jer vjerno prenosi klasične korake lectio divina (lectio, meditatio, oratio, contemplatio, actio) koje preporučuje Učiteljstvo.
magisterium.enrichment: Učiteljstvo naglašava da se Pismo mora čitati u zajedništvu Crkve kako bi se izbjegao individualizam. Također, proces nije dovršen bez 'actio' – konkretnog djelovanja u ljubavi koje preobražava svakodnevni život.

citations.document_title: The Interpretation of the Bible in the Church

magisterium.assessment_en: The described method is fully aligned with Church teaching because it faithfully conveys the classic steps of lectio divina (lectio, meditatio, oratio, contemplatio, actio) which are recommended by the Magisterium.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Magisterium emphasizes that Scripture must be read in the communion of the Church to avoid individualism. Also, the process is not complete without 'actio' – concrete action in love which transforms daily life.

sections.subtitle_en: Cleansing of the image of God through Scripture — step-by-step method
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina describes a concrete method of personal reading of the Gospel which corresponds to the classical lectio divina.
sections.content_en:
The host asks how to read Scripture and Brčina offers a method which in everything corresponds to the classical *lectio divina*, although he does not name it. First: 'God's word is not a newspaper nor a book. You pray nicely to the Holy Spirit, you go carefully, you read it once, then you return again, then a second and third time if needed.' Second: 'Listen — will your heart stir at that text, perhaps at some word, perhaps at the whole sentence, perhaps at the whole context.' Third: 'Return, ask — God, what does this word say to me? Why did my heart stop here now? What do You want to tell me through this word?' And fourth: 'Take that word and make a prayer of thanksgiving from it. And then take that word and live with it all day long.' He repeatedly emphasizes that Scripture 'best cleanses the image of God or what we imagined God to be. Now we can privately imagine God this way, that way, perhaps they imprinted an image on us in childhood — but one must listen to God's word because the image of God is revealed there.'

> **Magisterium alignment:** The method Brčina describes is structurally *lectio divina* (reading – meditation – prayer – contemplation/living), recommended in the post-synodal apostolic exhortation *Verbum Domini* (nos. 86-87) of Benedict XVI and in the apostolic constitution *Vultum Dei quaerere* of Pope Francis.

sections.subtitle: Zajednica vjere — Marija i Elizabeta kao model
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina poseže za biblijskom slikom dvije trudne žene koje se susreću kako bi pojasnio zašto su nam potrebni 'sudruzi po vjeri'.
sections.content:
Razgovor se prebacuje na zajednicu. Brčina ne govori apstraktno o crkvenoj zajednici, nego iz vrlo praktičnog kuta: 'Mi smo dar jedni drugima. Nitko od nas nije ni najpametniji, ni najljepši, ni najsposobniji. Mi smo svi dar jedni drugima, a svi imamo različite darove — i to je živa crkva.' Citira Knjigu Mudrosti: 'Pravi je prijatelj balzam života, pronalazi ga onaj koji se Boga boji, jer kakav čovjek, takav i njegov prijatelj.'

Biblijska slika je za njega ključ. 'Ja uvijek razmišljam — zašto je Elizabeta išla kod Marije kad je saznala da je trudna? Pa i ona je trudna bila — zašto su se morale sastati? Nije, dvije su se mogle razumjeti. Da je nekome drugom otišla, ne bi se bavili, rekli bi da je luda.' I mi, kaže, trebamo nekoga komu možemo govoriti o vjeri, problemima, čudima koja nam se događaju.

Na pitanje kako birati to društvo nudi praktičan odgovor: 'Ako si Božji i ako te Bog vodi — ne brini, ništa ne moraš birati. Svi oni loši će nestati oko tebe, Bog će to pročistiti. Ja to zovem prirodna duhovna selekcija. Odjedanput vidiš da su oni koji su se lažno pretvarali, koji su se kleli da će s tobom ostati do kraja, nestali u vidu magle.'

magisterium.assessment: Tekst izvrsno povezuje biblijski događaj Pohođenja s ekleziologijom darova i karizmi, naglašavajući komplementarnost u Tijelu Kristovu.
magisterium.enrichment: Pojam 'prirodna duhovna selekcija' može se produbiti kroz nauk o božanskoj Providnosti koja usmjerava vjernike prema zajedništvu (communio). Također, naglasak na Marijinoj vjeri kao pokretaču Elizabetina proročkog duha dodatno osvjetljava ulogu laika u poslanju Crkve prema Lumen Gentium 12.

citations.document_title: Iuvenescit Ecclesia - Hierarchical and Charismatic Gifts
citations.document_author: Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith

magisterium.assessment_en: The text excellently connects the biblical event of the Visitation with the ecclesiology of gifts and charisms, emphasizing complementarity in the Body of Christ.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The concept of 'natural spiritual selection' can be deepened through the teaching on divine Providence which directs the faithful towards communion (communio). Also, the emphasis on Mary's faith as the initiator of Elizabeth's prophetic spirit further illuminates the role of the laity in the mission of the Church according to Lumen Gentium 12.

sections.subtitle_en: Community of faith — Mary and Elizabeth as model
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina uses the biblical image of two pregnant women meeting in order to explain why we need 'companions in faith'.
sections.content_en: The conversation shifts to the community. Brčina does not speak abstractly about the church community, but from a very practical angle: 'We are a gift to each other. None of us is the smartest, nor the most beautiful, nor the most capable. We are all a gift to each other, and we all have different gifts — and that is a living church.' He quotes the Book of Wisdom: 'A true friend is a balm of life; he who fears God finds him, for as the man is, so is his friend.' The biblical image is key for him. 'I always think — why did Elizabeth go to Mary when she found out she was pregnant? Well, she was pregnant too — why did they have to meet? No, the two could understand each other. If she had gone to someone else, they wouldn't have bothered, they would have said she was crazy.' And we, he says, need someone to whom we can speak about faith, problems, miracles that happen to us. To the question of how to choose that company, he offers a practical answer: 'If you are God's and if God leads you — don't worry, you don't have to choose anything. All the bad ones will disappear around you, God will purify it. I call that natural spiritual selection. Suddenly you see that those who falsely pretended, who swore they would stay with you until the end, disappeared like fog.'

sections.subtitle: Vrijedim jer sam Božji — identitet i autentičnost
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina ide u izravnu raspravu o identitetu mladih i ovisnosti o tuđem priznanju.
sections.content:
Voditelj citira blaženog Karla Acutisa: 'Svi se rađamo kao originali, a mnogi umiremo kao kopije.' Brčina to razvija u kritiku današnjeg svijeta u kojem mladi mjere svoju vrijednost 'kroz prizmu i ogledalo drugih — što su mu rekli, kako ga drugi doživljavaju'. Zato su mnogi razočarani: 'Kad te ljudi izdaju, povrijede, kažu nešto ili razbiju sliku koju si ti izgradio, ti si u očaju.'

Alternativu formulira jednostavno: 'Kad je čovjek siguran u sebe i znaš da tvoj identitet, tvoja vrijednost ne mjeri tvoje riječi prema meni — ja vrijedim bez obzira me netko voli ili ne voli ili što misli o meni. Ja vrijedim jer sam Božji.' Voditelj dodaje da je to razlika između 'biti' i 'imati' — između originalnosti koja dolazi od Boga i 'neoriginalnosti' koja se gradi na auto-u, diplomi ili habitu.

magisterium.assessment: Izjava je u potpunosti usklađena s katoličkom antropologijom jer utemeljuje ljudsko dostojanstvo u ontološkoj stvarnosti slike Božje (imago Dei), a ne u promjenjivim društvenim percepcijama ili postignućima. Naglasak na 'biti' nasuprot 'imati' izravno odražava nauk o urođenom dostojanstvu koje osoba posjeduje samim svojim postojanjem kao Božje stvorenje.
magisterium.enrichment: Dokument 'Dignitas Infinita' potvrđuje da dostojanstvo proizlazi iz ljubavi Stvoritelja i da je ono 'neuvjetovano' te se ne može izgubiti. 'Gaudium et Spes' 22 produbljuje ovu misao ističući da Krist 'potpuno otkriva čovjeka njemu samome', čime autentičnost pronalazi svoj vrhunac u suobličenju Kristu koji je savršena Slika Božja.

citations.document_author: Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith

citations.document_title: Communion and Stewardship: Human Persons Created in the Image of God

magisterium.assessment_en: The statement is fully aligned with Catholic anthropology because it grounds human dignity in the ontological reality of the image of God (imago Dei), and not in changeable social perceptions or achievements. The emphasis on 'being' as opposed to 'having' directly reflects the teaching about the inherent dignity that a person possesses by their very existence as God's creature.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The document 'Dignitas Infinita' confirms that dignity stems from the love of the Creator and that it is 'unconditional' and cannot be lost. 'Gaudium et Spes' 22 deepens this thought by emphasizing that Christ 'fully reveals man to himself', whereby authenticity finds its culmination in conformity with Christ who is the perfect Image of God.

sections.subtitle_en: I am valuable because I am God's — identity and authenticity
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina goes into a direct discussion about the identity of young people and dependence on others' recognition.
sections.content_en: The host quotes Blessed Carlo Acutis: 'We are all born as originals, but many die as copies.' Brčina develops this into a critique of today's world in which young people measure their worth 'through the prism and mirror of others — what they told him, how others perceive him.' That is why many are disappointed: 'When people betray you, hurt you, say something or break the image you have built, you are in despair.' He formulates the alternative simply: 'When a person is sure of himself and you know that your identity, your worth does not measure your words towards me — I am worthy regardless of whether someone loves me or not or what they think of me. I am worthy because I am God's.' The host adds that this is the difference between 'being' and 'having' — between originality that comes from God and 'unoriginality' that is built on a car, a diploma, or a habit.

sections.subtitle: Napasti kao znak duhovnog života
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina obrće pitanje o duhovnoj borbi — ne treba se brinuti kad imaš kušnje, nego kad ih nemaš.
sections.content:
Na pitanje kako živjeti s napašću usprkos sakramentima, Brčina iznosi tezu koja preokreće uobičajeni pristup: 'Ja uvijek ljudima kažem da je to najbolja provjera naše autentičnosti i odnosa s Bogom. Jer nemoguće je da si povezan s Bogom, a da nemaš kušnji, napasti. Mislim da naglasak ne treba stavljati na to — naglasak treba staviti na one koji nemaju kušnje, probleme — da se zapitaju zašto nemaju.'

Logiku gradi izravno: 'Duhovno mrtve đavao nikad neće napasti — što će? Nego će napasti one ljude koji žele napraviti iskorak, koji se žele približiti Bogu. Hajde, odluči doći na antunovski hod; hajde, odluči otići na ispovijed; hajde, odluči učiniti neko dobro djelo, pa da vidiš kako udara đavao.'

Odmah relativizira pretjerivanja: 'Mnogi ljudi nekad pretjeruju s tim, vraga vide u pašteti. Vrag iskače iz svega, sve je vrag, vrag, vrag — pa neka ga, brate, on neka radi svoj posao, a mi radimo svoje. Bog je iznad svega.'

> **Magisterium uskladenost:** Brčinin balans odgovara nauku pape Franje u apostolskoj pobudnici *Gaudete et exsultate* (br. 158-162) gdje se duhovna borba prikazuje kao normalan dio kršćanskog života, ali se istovremeno odbacuje senzacionalizam i pretjerivanje. KKC 2847 govori o napastima kao 'razabiranju i borbi'.

magisterium.assessment: Izjava je u potpunosti usklađena s naukom Crkve o duhovnom boju, naglašavajući da je odsustvo kušnji znak duhovne mlakosti ili 'uspavanosti', dok istovremeno mudro odbacuje praznovjerni senzacionalizam.
magisterium.enrichment: Papa Franjo u 'Gaudete et exsultate' potvrđuje da je kršćanski život stalna borba protiv đavla, a ne samo protiv ljudskih slabosti, te da oni koji ne prepoznaju tu borbu postaju plijen osrednjosti. Crkva uči da je budnost 'čuvanje srca' neophodno jer đavao često dolazi prerušen u 'anđela svjetla' upravo kada smo previše sigurni u sebe.

citations.document_author: Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith

magisterium.assessment_en: The statement is fully aligned with the teaching of the Church on spiritual combat, emphasizing that the absence of temptations is a sign of spiritual lukewarmness or 'sleepiness', while simultaneously wisely rejecting superstitious sensationalism.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Pope Francis in 'Gaudete et exsultate' confirms that Christian life is a constant struggle against the devil, and not only against human weaknesses, and that those who do not recognize this struggle become prey to mediocrity. The Church teaches that vigilance, 'guarding the heart,' is essential because the devil often comes disguised as an 'angel of light' precisely when we are too sure of ourselves.

sections.subtitle_en: Temptations as a sign of spiritual life
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina reverses the question about spiritual battle — one should not worry when you have temptations, but when you don't have them.
sections.content_en:
To the question of how to live with temptation despite the sacraments, Brčina presents a thesis that overturns the usual approach: 'I always tell people that it is the best test of our authenticity and relationship with God. Because it is impossible to be connected with God and not have temptations. I think the emphasis should not be placed on that — the emphasis should be placed on those who do not have temptations, problems — to ask themselves why they do not have them.'

He builds the logic directly: 'The devil will never attack the spiritually dead — what would he do? Instead, he will attack those people who want to make a step forward, who want to draw closer to God. Come on, decide to come to the antunovski walk; come on, decide to go to confession; come on, decide to do some good deed, and then you will see how the devil strikes.'

He immediately relativizes exaggerations: 'Many people sometimes exaggerate with that, they see the devil in pâté. The devil jumps out of everything, everything is the devil, the devil, the devil — well, let him be, brother, let him do his job, and we do ours. God is above all.'

> **Magisterium alignment:** Brčina's balance corresponds to the teaching of Pope Francis in the apostolic exhortation *Gaudete et exsultate* (nos. 158-162) where spiritual struggle is presented as a normal part of Christian life, but at the same time sensationalism and exaggeration are rejected. CCC 2847 speaks of temptations as 'discernment and struggle'.

sections.subtitle: Mladi traže sadržaj, ne kritike
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina priča anegdotu o muškoj ekipi koja je u Kninu čekala duhovnu obnovu i prepoznala ga u kafiću.
sections.content:
Prema kraju razgovora Brčina prelazi na ono što vidi u stvarnom radu s mladima. Pita ga voditelj jesu li mladi 'digli ljestvicu' i može li je crkva zadovoljiti, a on odgovara da nije visoka jer 'traže Boga, traže ljubav — a Bog je ljubav — i traže mir'.

Ilustrira anegdotom iz Knina: dok je čekao župnika da otvori crkvu za duhovnu obnovu, ušao je u kafić; u WC-u ga je prepoznao čovjek, izašao je van i zatekao 'cijelu mušku ekipu — tata, jedan sin' kako se okupila da ga skupa fotografira. 'Prvi put u životu da sam našao mušku ekipu koja se okupila — još čeka drugog, još jednog koji treba doći — i ide na duhovnu obnovu. To u Hrvatskoj ne zvuči malo.'

Iz toga izvlači načelnu poruku za pastoralni rad: 'Mlade ne možemo kritizirati, mladima trebamo dati sadržaja. Sadržaja nikad malo, uvijek trebamo biti kreativni — što još, kako još. Ne možemo ih samo kritizirati — ne idu u crkvu, ne idu na ovo, ne idu na ono — a što smo mi njima dali, što smo im ponudili?'

magisterium.assessment: Izjava je u potpunom suglasju s postsinodalnom pobudnicom Christus vivit, naglašavajući potrebu za fleksibilnim, kreativnim i uključivim pastoralom koji izbjegava puku kritiku i nudi autentičan susret s Bogom.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva poziva na 'pučki' pastoral mladih koji otvara vrata svima, bez obzira na njihovu razinu vjerske poučenosti, fokusirajući se na kerygmu — radosnu vijest o Božjoj ljubavi. Potrebno je poticati mlade kao protagoniste evangelizacije koji vlastitim jezikom i kreativnošću naviještaju Krista svojim vršnjacima.
magisterium.assessment_en: The statement is in full agreement with the post-synodal apostolic exhortation Christus vivit, emphasizing the need for flexible, creative, and inclusive pastoral care which avoids mere criticism and offers an authentic encounter with God.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church calls for a 'popular' youth ministry that opens doors to everyone, regardless of their level of religious instruction, focusing on the kerygma — the joyful news of God's love. It is necessary to encourage young people as protagonists of evangelization who, with their own language and creativity, proclaim Christ to their peers.

sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina tells an anecdote about a group of men who were waiting in Knin for a spiritual renewal and recognized him in a cafe.
sections.content_en: Towards the end of the conversation, Brčina moves on to what he sees in real work with young people. The host asks him if young people have 'raised the bar' and if the church can meet it, and he replies that it is not high because 'they seek God, they seek love — and God is love — and they seek peace'. He illustrates with an anecdote from Knin: while waiting for the parish priest to open the church for a spiritual renewal, he entered a cafe; in the restroom, a man recognized him, went outside and found 'an entire male crew — a father, one son' gathered to photograph him together. 'For the first time in my life, I found a male crew that gathered — still waiting for another, one more who needs to come — and is going to a spiritual renewal. That doesn't sound insignificant in Croatia.' From this, he draws a general message for pastoral work: 'We cannot criticize young people, we need to give young people content. Content is never scarce, we always need to be creative — what else, how else. We cannot just criticize them — they don't go to church, they don't go to this, they don't go to that — but what have we given them, what have we offered them?'

sections.subtitle: Franjevački kamp — novi pastoralni iskorak
sections.screenshot_description: Brčina najavljuje izgradnju Franjevačkog kampa kao produžetak pastorala sa Svetog Duha.
sections.content: Konkretan izraz tog pristupa najava je akcije prikupljanja sredstava za izgradnju franjevačkog kampa. Brčina objašnjava da svake četiri godine — koliko mu traje mandat — promišlja što novo evangelizacijski ponuditi. 'Mnogi mladi gore koji dolaze su potrebni dodatnog sadržaja. Mi ne selimo pastoral koji već imamo nigdje — on stoji dalje na Svetom Duhu — nego produžetak.' Detalje ne otkriva, ali kao mjeru potrebe navodi da kroz osam godina svih projekata — od redovitih okupljanja u crkvi, franjevačkih kampova na moru, proslave Valentinova, do Tečaja Filip — 'samo je falilo mjesto'.

magisterium.assessment: Inicijativa je u potpunosti usklađena s pozivom na 'novu evangelizaciju' i kreativno promišljanje pastoralnih metoda koje nadilaze tradicionalne okvire. Projekt utjelovljuje franjevačku karizmu kroz neposredan kontakt s ljudima i stvaranje prostora za zajedništvo, što je ključno za autentično svjedočenje evanđelja mladima.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva potiče da mladi ne budu samo primatelji, već i aktivni protagonisti evangelizacije, koristeći vlastiti jezik i kreativnost u takvim kampovima. Važno je osigurati da ovaj 'prostor' postane mjesto 'poljske bolnice' gdje se kroz duhovnu pratnju i sakramente iscjeljuju rane i gradi duboko povjerenje u Boga.

citations.document_title: To the Order of the Friars Minor Conventual

magisterium.assessment_en: The initiative is fully aligned with the call for 'new evangelization' and creative rethinking of pastoral methods that transcend traditional frameworks. The project embodies the Franciscan charism through direct contact with people and the creation of space for community, which is crucial for authentic witnessing of the Gospel to young people.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church encourages young people not to be only recipients, but also active protagonists of evangelization, using their own language and creativity in such camps. It is important to ensure that this 'space' becomes a place of a 'field hospital' where through spiritual accompaniment and sacraments wounds are healed and deep trust in God is built.

sections.subtitle_en: Franciscan camp — new pastoral step forward
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brčina announces the construction of the Franciscan camp as an extension of the pastoral care from Sveti Duh.
sections.content_en: A concrete expression of that approach is the announcement of a fundraising campaign for the construction of a Franciscan camp. Brčina explains that every four years — for the duration of his mandate — he considers what new to offer evangelization-wise. 'Many young people who come here need additional content. We are not moving the pastoral care we already have anywhere — it remains at Sveti Duh — but an extension.' He does not reveal details, but as a measure of need he states that through eight years of all projects — from regular gatherings in the church, Franciscan camps by the sea, Valentine's Day celebrations, to Tečaj Filip — 'only a place was missing'.

sections.subtitle: Aplikacija, ruta i poziv: 6. lipnja u 12 sati
sections.screenshot_description: Završne tehničke informacije o osmom Antunovskom hodu i pozivu na sudjelovanje.
sections.content:
Razgovor završava praktičnim informacijama. Brčina pozdravlja sve koji se 'osjećaju mladima' i napominje da je ruta ove godine prilagođena svima — 'nitko ne može reći da mu je predugo'. Predstavlja novost: aplikaciju Antunovskog hoda *Mladi tak* koja nudi sve informacije o hodu, ruti, mjestima odakle ljudi dolaze i molitvama Svetom Antunu. Tko je preuzme na vrijeme, ulazi u tri nagradne igre — hodočašće u Padovu, ljetovanje u franjevačkom kampu za mlade na Cresu i paket opreme za hod.

Voditelj sažima logistiku: osmi Antunovski hod, 6. lipnja (subota), 12 sati predprogram, 14 sati sveta misa koju predslavi fra Stjepan, zatim hod do trga, molitva s evangelizacijskom zajednicom i euharistijsko klanjanje. Na završetku na Svetom Duhu sudionike će dočekati biskup s blagoslovom. 'Eto, sad smo obuhvatili sve. I laike i hijerarhiju.'

magisterium.assessment: Program izvrsno korelira s crkvenim smjernicama jer postavlja Euharistiju kao 'vrhunac i stožer' pastoralnog djelovanja, integrirajući hod kao snažan čin evangelizacije i profesije vjere. Struktura vjerno odražava narav Crkve kao hodočasničkog naroda koji se okuplja oko biskupa i sakramenata.
magisterium.enrichment: Preporučuje se eksplicitno uključivanje sakramenta pomirenja u predprogram, budući da je pomirenje s Bogom i bližnjima temeljni cilj svakog hodočašća. Također, nagradne igre poput hodočašća u Padovu mogu se teološki uokviriti kao poticaj na daljnji rast u karizmi svetog Antuna i 'pedagogiju evangelizacije'.

citations.document_title: Directory on Popular Piety and the Liturgy

magisterium.assessment_en: The program excellently correlates with Church guidelines because it sets the Eucharist as 'the summit and pivot' of pastoral activity, integrating the walk as a powerful act of evangelization and profession of faith. The structure faithfully reflects the nature of the Church as a pilgrim people who gathers around bishops and sacraments.
magisterium.enrichment_en: It is recommended to explicitly include the sacrament of reconciliation in the pre-program, since reconciliation with God and neighbors is the fundamental goal of every pilgrimage. Also, prize games such as a pilgrimage to Padova can be theologically framed as an incentive for further growth in the charism of Saint Anthony and 'pedagogy of evangelization'.

sections.subtitle_en: Application, route and call: June 6 at 12 PM
sections.screenshot_description_en: Final technical information about the eighth Antunovski hod and invitation to participate.
sections.content_en: The conversation ends with practical information. Brčina greets everyone who 'feels young' and notes that this year's route is adapted for everyone — 'no one can say it's too long for them'. He presents a novelty: the Antunovski hod application *Mladi tak* which offers all information about the walk, the route, the places people come from, and prayers to Saint Anthony. Whoever downloads it on time, enters three prize games — a pilgrimage to Padova, a summer vacation in a Franciscan youth camp on Cres, and a package of equipment for the walk. The host summarizes the logistics: the eighth Antunovski hod, June 6 (Saturday), 12 PM pre-program, 2 PM Holy Mass presided over by fra Stjepan, then a walk to the square, prayer with the evangelization community and Eucharistic adoration. At the end, at Sveti Duh, the participants will be greeted by the bishop with a blessing. 'Well, now we have covered everything. Both the laity and the hierarchy.'

iterations.theme_en: Personal prayer of the heart, God's word, community and challenges of youth ministry
